summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes obfuscated Windows binary, then self-erases
details: On npm install, the package's postinstall hook (install-hook.js) executes a base64/UTF-16LE-encoded PowerShell command that downloads launcher.bin from https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin, XOR-decodes the bytes with key 0x42, writes the result to %TEMP%\tmp.exe, and launches it via Start-Process -WindowStyle Hidden. The publisher account (Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note) is unrelated to the package's stated purpose (email verification), and the payload is delivered as an opaque, obfuscated binary from a mutable release asset. Immediately after spawning the dropper, install-hook.js writes and detaches a cleanup_<ts>.js script that, after a brief delay, deletes the mailconfirmer entry from the consumer's package.json and package-lock.json and recursively removes the module's own folder from node_modules — anti-forensics intended to hide the compromise from post-incident review. Any Windows machine running `npm install` against this package is compromised.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
